title: legousia falcata subsp. castellana
intro: Legousia falcata subsp. castellana, also known as Castellana gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an expectorant. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as iridoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, typically in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
